description_long: This is a traditional Japanese samurai helmet, known as a kabuto, featuring a prominent pair of large, upward-curving golden crests (maedate) at the front. The helmet bowl is constructed from overlapping metal plates, giving it a ribbed, segmented appearance, and is finished in a dark metallic color with subtle blue highlights along the seams. Decorative gold motifs are present on the side flaps, which are attached to the main bowl and provide additional protection for the sides of the head. A maroon cord is attached at the base, designed to secure the helmet under the chin. The surface of the helmet is mostly smooth with a slightly glossy finish, and the overall design is both functional and highly ornamental, reflecting its ceremonial and protective purposes. The interior is likely lined for comfort, though this is not visible from the exterior views.
description: A traditional Japanese samurai kabuto helmet with large golden crests, ribbed dark metal plates, blue highlights, and decorative side flaps, secured by a maroon chin cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large golden crests, decorative motifs on the side flaps, and the maroon chin cord.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the ribbed metal construction, blue seam highlights, and the profile of the golden crest.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the segmented, overlapping plates and the full width of the helmet's protective structure.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the ribbed plates, blue highlights, and the side profile of the crest.
attribute_graphic_design: Decorative gold motifs on the side flaps and intricate patterns along the front rim; overall design is ornate and traditional.
